title: Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani

seo.title: How to Make Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani — Easy Indian Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani. This easy Indian recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.
longTailKeywords: how to make Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani
easy Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani recipe
authentic Indian Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani
lowCompetitionKeywords: Indian Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani recipe
seo.h1: Authentic Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani Recipe
h2Headings: What is Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani?
Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: Authentic Indian Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani recipe
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani — a beloved Indian classic

description: Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani, is a classic Indian dish renowned for its rich, creamy tomato gravy and tender chicken. This iconic recipe delivers a perfect balance of smoky, tangy, and subtly sweet flavors, making it a beloved comfort food. Enjoy this delightful curry with naan or rice for an unforgettable meal.

ingredients.notes: soaked in warm water for 15 minutes

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine chicken pieces, yogurt, 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste, 1/4 tsp turmeric powder, 1/2 tsp red chili powder, 1/2 tsp garam masala, 1/2 tsp cumin powder, and 1/2 tsp salt. Mix well to coat the chicken thoroughly. Marinate for at least 30 minutes at room temperature, or preferably 2 hours to overnight in the refrigerator for best flavor.

instructions.title: Cook the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat 1 tbsp butter and 1 tbsp vegetable oil in a large heavy-bottomed pan or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces in batches, making sure not to overcrowd the pan. Cook until browned and lightly charred on the outside, about 5-7 minutes per batch. Remove the cooked chicken from the pan and set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Gravy Base
instructions.text: In the same pan, add another 1 tbsp butter and 1 tbsp vegetable oil. Add the finely chopped onion and cook over medium heat until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Stir in the remaining 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste and the slit green chilies (if using), cooking for 1-2 minutes until fragrant.

instructions.title: Simmer the Tomato Gravy
instructions.text: Pour in the tomato puree or crushed tomatoes. Add the remaining 1/4 tsp turmeric powder, 1/2 tsp red chili powder, 1/2 tsp garam masala, 1/2 tsp cumin powder, and 1/2 tsp salt. Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer. Add the soaked and drained cashews. Cover the pan and cook for 15-20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce thickens and the oil begins to separate from the edges.

instructions.title: Blend for Smoothness
instructions.text: Carefully transfer the hot gravy mixture to a blender. Blend until it is completely smooth and creamy. For an exceptionally smooth restaurant-style texture, pass the blended gravy through a fine-mesh sieve back into the clean pan, pressing down on any solids. Discard any fibrous residue.

instructions.title: Combine and Finish
instructions.text: Return the smooth gravy to the pan over low-medium heat. Add the previously cooked chicken pieces to the gravy. Stir in the heavy cream, the remaining 2 tbsp butter, crushed Kasoori Methi, and sugar. Simmer gently for another 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the chicken to absorb the rich sauce. Taste and adjust salt or sweetness as needed.

instructions.title: Garnish and Serve
instructions.text: Garnish the Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani generously with fresh chopped coriander leaves. Serve hot with warm naan bread, roti, or fluffy basmati rice for a truly authentic Indian dining experience.

tips: For the most tender chicken, marinate it for at least 2 hours or overnight. The yogurt helps to tenderize the meat and infuse it with flavor.
Always blend the gravy until completely smooth. Passing it through a sieve after blending ensures a silky, restaurant-quality texture that is characteristic of authentic Murgh Makhani.
Kasoori Methi (dried fenugreek leaves) is a crucial ingredient for authentic Butter Chicken flavor. Crush it between your palms before adding to release its aromatic oils.
article: Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani is a beloved Indian Lunch Recipes dish enjoyed across Indian. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
